contribution.title: Poincare conjecture (later proved by Perelman)
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Indirectly relevant; topology of spatial slices matters for global GR claims.

contribution.title: Foundations of dynamical systems and chaos theory
contribution.description: Showed that the three-body problem can exhibit sensitive dependence on initial conditions, prefiguring chaos theory.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Directly relevant for Lyapunov-exponent and chaos modules in CHAOS_STATS.

contribution.title: Poincare sections
contribution.description: Technique for analyzing periodic and quasi-periodic orbits via successive intersections with a transversal surface.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Implemented as poincare_section_count in the TRAJECTORIES schema.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Qualitative methods: identify invariants and topology before computing.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: Is the long-term behavior periodic, quasi-periodic, or chaotic?
What are the fixed points and their stability?
What is the topology of the phase space?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Sometimes prioritized intuition over careful computation; verify quantitatively.

reference.citation: Poincare, H. (1890). Sur le probleme des trois corps et les equations de la dynamique. Acta Mathematica 13, 1.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Analyzing trajectory chaos, ergodicity, or long-term stability of geodesics.
how_to_apply.cautions: Chaos diagnostics need long integrations and careful error control to be meaningful.
